title: Single-Stranded Binding Protein (SSB)
summary: A protein that coats single-stranded DNA at the structures/replication-fork to prevent it from re-annealing into a double helix or forming hairpin secondary structures. Keeps the template accessible for enzymes/dna-polymerase-iii|DNA polymerase III. Strictly a "binding protein" — not catalytically an enzyme — but treated alongside enzymes in the replication context.
